summary: Full delivery of the Todo List App — a lightweight React + localStorage prototype built and deployed to staging in one pipeline run. No backend, no external dependencies.
why: Client needed a fast MVP to validate UX assumptions before investing in a backend. Goal: ship something testable in hours, not days.
how: PM Discovery captured 4 MoSCoW user stories. Architect defined the stack (React + localStorage, no backend) and file structure. Developer built 3 components + useTasks hook. CTO Reviewer audited the code (2 non-blocking findings). QA validated the full flow. Deploy agent built and rsynced to staging.
outcome: App live on staging. All 4 user stories covered. Bundle: 18.43 kB gzipped. Zero critical issues. Ready for client demo.
qaSteps: Full flow tested manually: add task → toggle done/todo → filter → reload (persistence OK). Staging URL access confirmed post-deploy.
